title: TSB and LB Media
steps: Calculate the weight of powder needed based on the amount of media you wish to make. When considering how much volume you need, remember to leave at least 100 mL headroom in your container– this to prevent spillage in the autoclave.

As of 2024/09/09, the standard concentration for TSB and LB are as follows: Tryptic Soy Broth (TSB): 30 g / 1 L Luria Base (LB): 25 g / 1 L For example, if you wanted 200 mL of TSB you would calculate: 30 g * ( 200 mL / 1000 mL )= 6 g TSB powder (per 200 mL media)
Using a white or metal spatula and a plastic weigh boat that have all been sterilized with ethanol, measure out the amount of powder needed for your media.
Remember to tare the weight of the weigh boat before measuring out the powder. Do not over portion– we should not be putting powder back into the stock bottle.
Add your powder to the bottle first. Then use a graduated cylinder to measure and add your liquid.
Shake the bottle until all the powder is dissolved.
Cut a piece of colorful tape and fold over the end to make a tab. Write the name of your media, today's date (YY/MM/DD), and your initials. For example for TSB: "TSB 24/09/09 JF"
Autoclave using the Liquid15 cycle and smile at your success :)
Follow the same instructions above for making liquid media, but now at step 3, include the desired amount of agar.

Check the bottle of agar for concentration specification, but generally a 1.5% agar solution is desired which equates to 15 g / 1 L For example, if you wanted 200 mL of 1.5% TSB agar (aka TSA), you would calculate 15 g * ( 200 mL / 1000 mL )= 3 g agar (per 200 mL TSB media)
creator: Jaycee Fahrner
